attach_too_large: File too large ({size} MB, max {max} MB). Reduce it (e.g. compress images) and try again.
preferences_name_desc: The name shown in the user list and the account menu. You can change it at any time.
preferences_name_note: Leave empty to show only your email.
preferences_language_desc: Choose the interface language. “App default” follows the administrator setting.
preferences_language_note: Changing the language reloads the page once.
preferences_interface_desc: Enable or hide interface elements. Greyed options are locked by the administrator.
files_description: Every file exchanged across your conversations (sent and received).
files_delete_confirm_desc: It will be removed from your files. The conversation where it was exchanged is unchanged.
appearance_default_theme_desc: Applied to users without a personal preference.
appearance_default_language_desc: Language for new users who haven’t picked their own yet.
appearance_default_language_note: If you haven’t set your own language, changing this default will reload your view.
appearance_admin_section_desc: Admins only. Applies to the whole organization.
charts_user_section_desc: Pick the palette applied to your interface, in light and dark.
charts_admin_default_desc: Applied to users without a personal chart.
charts_admin_availability_desc: Define each chart's pool: the groups that MAY offer it. A group manager then selects from its pool (Groups tab). A chart stays common until a group actually selects it.
charts_admin_domain_desc: Map a domain to a public chart (builtin or common): it becomes the default chart on that host, including the login page.
charts_import_section_desc: Paste a chart's JSON (or pick a .json file) to create your personal chart. Colors must be in oklch format; values are validated and sanitized server-side.
charts_import_json_placeholder: Paste the tokens JSON here (colors.light / colors.dark in oklch, radius, fontSans, fontMono)…
charts_import_invalid_json: Invalid JSON: could not parse the content.
charts_mine_section_desc: The charts you have imported. You can rename them, edit their tokens, associate them with your groups, or delete them.
charts_mine_empty: You haven't imported any chart yet.
charts_mine_logo_help: Shown in the top bar when this chart is active. Any format (PNG, SVG, AVIF…) is converted to an optimized WebP.
charts_logo_err_not_image: The file must be an image.
charts_mine_domains_help: When Atrium is opened on this domain, this chart applies by default (including the login page). E.g. chat.acme.com or *.acme.com.
charts_admin_custom_section_desc: Manage charts imported by users: promote to common (offered to everyone) or delete.
pref_showSource_help: The </> button that shows a message’s exact raw text.
pref_showReport_help: The flag that sends feedback about a message.
pref_showTools_help: Shows tool activity and sources in the conversation (analysis view, with expandable detail). Off: a clean, content-focused view — the “processing in progress” signal stays visible.
pref_voiceInput_help: The mic button in the composer.
pref_showChatAge_help: Show each conversation’s age (e.g. “3d”, “2w”) in the left list.
pref_showChatProvider_help: Tag each conversation’s bridge (OpenClaw / Hermes) in the left list. Shown only if your conversations use more than one bridge.
prefs_dialog_desc: Show or hide interface elements. Some options depend on a system feature that isn’t enabled yet.
uiprefs_intro: Configure the interface: enable system features and set the defaults users inherit (each can then override them in their own preferences).
uiprefs_gates_note: A feature not enabled here cannot be turned on by users (greyed out).
uiprefs_gate_voiceInput_help: Enables the voice pipeline. While off, the option stays greyed and users can’t turn it on.
serviceaccounts_hint: Service accounts for OpenClaw agents (API key auth). A key's plaintext is shown only once at creation — it is never stored nor retrievable afterwards.
serviceaccounts_edit_description: Update the name, role and description.
serviceaccounts_sheet_description: Principal authenticated by API key (never a human).
serviceaccounts_delete_desc_after: will be permanently deleted. This action is irreversible. Type the account name to confirm.
serviceaccounts_revoke_desc_after: will be disabled immediately and will no longer be able to authenticate requests. This action is irreversible.
serviceaccounts_minted_description: For the account « {name} ». Copy it now.
serviceaccounts_minted_warning: Store this key in a safe place. You will never see it again. To replace it, you will have to revoke it and generate a new one.
chat_empty_select: Select or create a chat to begin.
chat_notfound_body: This link does not match any conversation. It may have been deleted, or the address is incomplete.
chat_unavailable_banner: The chat service is temporarily unavailable. Sending messages is suspended — try again in a moment. If this persists, contact your administrator.
chat_readonly_banner: This chat is read-only: its agent is no longer available to you (your agent access was changed). Start a new chat with an available agent.
chat_agent_select_title: Choose the agent for the next message
chat_agent_select_aria: Choose the agent for the next message
chat_agent_select_firstturn_hint: This conversation's agent is set with the first message; choose a different agent from the second message onward.
chat_thinking_inherited_title: Thinking level inherited from the agent
chat_delete_assistant_desc: This reply will be deleted, then regenerated from your last message.
chat_delete_user_title: Delete this message and the following ones?
chat_delete_user_desc: This message and all messages following it will be deleted from the conversation. This action is irreversible.
chat_delete_user_btn_title: Delete this message and the following ones
sources_trigger_aria: View the sources of the reply
sources_attach_help: Fetches the checked documents' source files as downloadable links (the "Original source" slot).
sources_attached_badge: {count} attached document(s) available for download — open the Sources panel
sources_attach_dispatched: Document fetch started — see the Sources panel
sources_attach_error: Could not start the document fetch
chat_run_taking_longer_agent: The agent {name} is processing your message…
chat_tools_hide: Hide tool activity and return to the clean view
chat_tools_show: Show tool activity (completed and in-progress processing)
tools_output_not_transmitted: Output not transmitted by the gateway
tools_field_omitted: Large content ({size} KB) — not shown here to keep loading fast.
delivery_hint: Measures the bridge → Convex → frontend pipeline per delta (timestamps only, no content).
delivery_no_samples: No samples for this session yet.
delivery_kpi_empty: Not enough recordings to plot a trend.
delivery_b_note: B (Convex exec): from Convex telemetry (not measurable in-app).
delivery_report_note: C is sampled at the browser render rate (≤ A).
delivery_truncated: Report capped: stats cover only the first rows of the window.
chat_queue_full: Too many messages waiting. Wait for the current reply to finish.
chat_queue_failed: Could not queue the message. Try again.
integrations_intro_after: — they live in the deployment environment; an empty field falls back to the environment variable then the default. The "configured" indicator reflects the presence of the key on the server side.
integrations_tts_note: Audio playback of replies. Provider key via env (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY).
integrations_talk_note_key_present: OpenAI realtime: OPENAI_API_KEY present (env). The browser will receive an ephemeral token minted server-side — never the raw key.
integrations_talk_note_key_absent: OpenAI realtime: OPENAI_API_KEY ABSENT (env). To be set on the deployment side to enable gpt-realtime.
integrations_talk_interrupt: Interrupt playback when the user speaks
integrations_voicewake_note: OpenClaw handles voice-wake on the Gateway side with native macOS/iOS detection (Android = manual mic); the browser is not a supported platform. Wiring it into this webchat would require a client-side wake-word engine (Porcupine-style) that we would build — outside OpenClaw. Trigger configuration: gateway RPC
integrations_cursors_empty: No cursor — no trace has been shipped yet.
feedbacks_reply_placeholder: Reply to the user (visible in their notification area)…
feedbacks_extensions_detected_title: Correction extensions detected (may alter the text)
feedbacks_hint_prefix: Recorded "Report Feedback" reports. The list shows metadata; opening the detail reads the frozen content and
feedbacks_hint_audit_strong: traces this access in the Audit
feedbacks_hint_suffix: (reading another user's content). Bounded recent window.
feedbacks_delete_confirm_description: The frozen forensic snapshot will be permanently deleted. This action is irreversible.
settings_delete_user_desc: “{user}” and all their data (conversations, files, access) will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
settings_instances_hint_after: (never entered by hand). Gateway credentials (token, device identity, API key) are set per instance via “Credentials” — encrypted at rest.
settings_name_locked_hint: The name is the routing key and can't be changed after creation.
settings_field_gateway_http_url_ph: Only if media is served on another host/port
settings_credentials_hint: Stored encrypted at rest. Write-only: a value is never shown again — set or replace it, or clear it.
settings_secret_generate_hint: Mints an Ed25519 identity server-side (the private key never reaches the browser) and saves it. You'll then pair it on the gateway.
settings_secret_pair_hint: Pair this device on your gateway with:
settings_secret_generate_failed: Could not generate the device identity
settings_sync_no_agents: No agents retrieved — pair the device on the gateway (or check the instance configuration), then sync again
settings_sync_err_no_bridge_url: No bridge serves this instance — set its “Bridge URL” in Settings → Agents → Instances.
settings_sync_err_deploy: Convex deployment is misconfigured: BRIDGE_SHARED_SECRET is missing.
settings_sync_err_unreachable: Bridge unreachable — check it is running and its URL is correct.
settings_sync_err_unauthorized: The bridge rejected authentication — BRIDGE_SHARED_SECRET is out of sync between Convex and the bridge.
settings_sync_err_not_served: The bridge does not serve this instance — its secret must be in BRIDGE_INSTANCE_SECRETS and its credentials must be valid.
settings_sync_hint: Forces the bridge to take the credentials into account and fetch the agents from the gateway now (otherwise ~2 min via the cron). Also triggers pairing if the device is not yet paired.
settings_bridge_secret_hint: Authenticates THIS bridge to Convex and ties it to this instance, so the bridge can only ever read this gateway's secrets. Set it in the bridge's environment. Shown once at generation.
settings_bridge_secret_mint_failed: Could not generate the bridge secret
settings_bridge_secret_reveal_warn: Copy this secret now — it is never shown again. Put it in the bridge's environment variable.
settings_manage_agents_hint: Agents are discovered by the bridge. Tick the ones to make available downstream (assignable to groups/users), and pick one default. A disabled agent stays listed but greyed.
settings_agent_types_pop_hint: An agent may hold several types. Each type tells Atrium how the agent can be used.
agent_type_conversational_desc: Standard chat agent — answers users' messages in a conversation.
agent_type_documentary_desc: Specialised agent invoked by a dedicated Atrium action to exploit a documentary source — not used for direct chat.
settings_remove_agent_desc: This agent no longer exists on the gateway. Removing it also removes it from every group's and user's agent list. This cannot be undone.
settings_audit_hint: Trace of actions performed under impersonation: who really acted (real actor) and on behalf of which user. Message content is never recorded.
settings_audit_window_hint: Bounded recent window — an earlier range may be partial.
roles_phi_tooltip: {key} — access to raw content (PHI), grant with caution
roles_confirm_edit_builtin_desc: Built-in roles are the foundation of the permission model. Editing them may affect existing accounts. Continue?
roles_hint_part1: Permission matrix. Rows = roles (built-in + custom), columns = permission keys. The
roles_hint_part2: role holds the “*” wildcard (all permissions) and is locked. The
roles_hint_part3: column grants access to raw content (PHI) — grant only when justified.
role_desc_pending: Authenticated but not yet approved — access is blocked until an admin approves the account.
role_desc_admin: Full access (superset): manages users, roles, agents, instances and observability.
role_desc_observer: READ-ONLY service account for monitoring: views traces, KPIs, anomalies and bridge/version status through the API. Cannot write or trigger anything.
role_desc_agent: Service account for an OpenClaw agent itself: the observer's read access PLUS querying OpenClaw and REPORTING anomalies (write — e.g. heartbeat / self-repair). Use it for an automated agent, not a human watching dashboards.
roles_sheet_desc: Custom role. Adjust the permissions in the matrix after creation.
anomalies_resolve_confirm_desc_after: It will be removed from the open anomalies count (OpenClaw self-repair signal).
anomalies_hint_intro: Anomalies detected (cron) or reported by OpenClaw agents. Non-PHI data only (type, severity, message, correlation).
anomalies_hint_open_desc: = active, it alerts and counts in the heartbeat.
anomalies_hint_muted_desc: = seen, no more alerts, but not necessarily fixed (admins are notified of new anomalies).
anomalies_hint_resolved_desc: = the problem is fixed (the detector resolves automatically when the condition clears). Both remove it from the heartbeat count.
anomalies_hint_time_window: The time range filters the recent window — an earlier range may be partial.
feedbackdlg_desc_submitted: Thank you. A complete snapshot of this message has been frozen for analysis.
feedbackdlg_desc_user: Report an issue with your message as it was sent. An exact forensic snapshot is recorded.
feedbackdlg_desc_assistant: Report an issue with this response. An exact forensic snapshot is recorded at send time.
feedbackdlg_fidelity_ok: ✓ The displayed text (source view) matches exactly, character for character, the text stored on the server. The comparison is frozen as evidence.
feedbackdlg_fidelity_warn: ⚠ Discrepancy detected between the displayed text (source view) and the stored text. The snapshot has frozen both versions.
feedbackdlg_fidelity_closed: Snapshot recorded. To verify that the display does not alter any character, open the source view (</>) on this message then report again: the comparison will then be on the text actually rendered by the browser.
feedbackdlg_btn_aria: Report an issue with this message
traces_hint_before: Recent events (bounded window, newest first). All traces are redacted metadata
traces_hint_after: : no message content, attachment or token is stored — only lengths, codes and flags.
traces_hint_window: The time range filters the recent window — an older range may be partial.
traces_empty_correlation: No event for this correlation in the window.
traces_meta_dialog_description: Non-PHI metadata only (lengths, codes, flags). No message content is stored.
app_signin_refused: Sign-in refused. This account’s email domain is not allowed.
app_pending_body: Your account has been created but must be approved by an administrator before you can access the chat.
app_imp_prefix: You are exploring the application as
app_imp_middle: . Every action is executed and traced in your name (
app_access_denied_body: You do not have permission to view this tab. Choose an available tab from the menu, or contact your administrator.
app_route_error_body: This page could not be displayed. Try again, or return home. If the problem persists, contact your administrator.
app_not_found_body: This address does not match any page in the application.
useraccess_dialog_description: Assign the discovered agents (per instance) and designate the default agent. At least one agent is required to create conversations.
useraccess_pool_group: The member's group agents. Check to restrict to a selection; with none checked, the member sees all of them.
useraccess_pool_all: The member belongs to no group, so they see every agent. Check to restrict to a selection.
useraccess_no_instances: No instance configured. Add one in the Instances tab.
useraccess_toast_set_default_denied: Setting the default agent was denied
bridge_config_hint: Non-secret bridge config, applied hot on the next message (no restart). Secrets (tokens, device identity) live in the bridge environment, never here.
bridge_config_no_instances: No instances yet — add one under Agents › Instances first.
bridge_config_media_mode_help: How the bridge fetches OUTBOUND (agent-generated) files to host them: gateway-http (over HTTP from the gateway — no shared filesystem needed, the portable default), shared-fs (read a mounted shared volume), or off (no outbound attachments).
bridge_config_inbound_mode_help: How user-uploaded files reach the agent: inline (base64 in the chat frame — capped by the gateway frame size), or shared-fs (streamed to a shared volume and referenced by path — any size, for large videos/audio/docs).
instance_transport_hint: How the browser receives the live reply for this instance's chats (frontend↔Convex — not a bridge setting): reactive (Convex's native push — best concurrency, the default) or sse (standard Server-Sent-Events / streamable-HTTP — multi-device, but on managed Convex Cloud each stream holds a concurrent-action slot, capped at 64/512).
bridge_config_media_max_mb_help: Per-file size cap (MB). In shared-fs mode this can be large (files stream to disk); for inline files the gateway frame size is the real limit.
bridge_config_rehydration_help: On a fresh/rolled gateway session, re-send prior turns so the model's context matches the visible thread. Disable to always send only the current message.
injection_section_help: Atrium splices a few standing instructions into each gateway turn so features work out of the box. If your gateway already instructs the agents, disable or customize them here so the turn's context isn't bloated for nothing.
injection_media_delivery_help: Tells the agent how to make a generated file downloadable in this webchat: write it to the outbound dir, then emit a MEDIA:<path> line. Disable ONLY if your agents are already instructed to emit MEDIA: — otherwise files they generate won't be downloadable. The outbound-directory placeholder (shown below) is filled automatically.
injection_documentary_fetch_help: The brief sent to the documentary agent for "Attach source documents": resolve the references to files and deliver them. Tailor it to how documents are reachable in YOUR environment — this is the prompt that makes or breaks the fetch. Disable to send ONLY the bare reference list (no Atrium framing) — for a gateway whose agent is already instructed to fetch references it receives. The references placeholder (shown below) is filled with the requested documents.
injection_inbound_files_help: The header shown to the agent before the list of files a user attached, so it can read them by path. Disable if your agents already know where received files are staged. The file-list placeholder (shown below) is filled with the staged files.
injection_tab_no_instances: No instance yet. Add one in the Instances tab first.
injection_preview_note: Placeholders filled with example values — what the agent receives.
injection_preview_disabled_empty: Disabled — nothing is injected into the agent's message.
placeholder_outboundDir: The agent's outbound directory — where it must write a generated file so the bridge can host it as a download.
placeholder_references: The list of requested document references (one per line) — what the documentary agent must resolve to files.
placeholder_files: The list of files the user attached, with their agent-visible paths and sizes — what the agent can read.
bridge_config_inbound_path_help: The path INSIDE the gateway container where the agent READS staged inbound files — the gateway's mount of the shared volume (e.g. /home/node/.openclaw/media/inbound). Must match your Docker volume mount.
bridge_config_outbound_path_help: The path INSIDE the gateway container where the agent WRITES a generated file before emitting MEDIA:<path> (e.g. /home/node/.openclaw/media/outbound). Must be writable by the agent in the container.
bridge_config_check_note: Checks the BRIDGE's access to the shared dirs. The agent-side mount (inside the gateway container) must be verified separately.
bridge_config_check_errored: Bridge reachable but the check failed ({reason})
bridge_health_hint: Bridge health — active poll every minute. Non-secret data only: reachability, connection states, last failure cause. Secrets (tokens, device identity) live in the bridge environment, never here.
bridge_no_reading_yet: No reading yet — the poll runs every minute. Check back in a moment.
bridge_no_connection_tested: No connection tested yet. A connection's state appears as soon as a message exercises it (the bridge connects on demand).
bridge_target_downstream_reject: Rejected by the gateway: {label} — not a bridge fault.
kpi_header_hint: Hourly aggregated indicators from redacted traces (non-PHI metadata only). Live updates (useQuery) — the rollup runs every hour.
kpi_header_range_note: The range filters the rollup history — an earlier range may be partial.
kpi_empty: No KPI data — the rollup runs every hour; run it or wait.
devswitch_dialog_description: Change roles and “act as” to test multiple users without the CLI. Visible in dev only.
devswitch_empty_state: No other account. Sign out then sign back in (new anonymous identity) — it will be active immediately.
devswitch_actas_pending_blocked: A pending account cannot be impersonated
sidebar_readonly_label: Read-only — its agent is no longer available to you
sidebar_delete_project_confirm_desc: This action is irreversible: it will also permanently delete {count} conversation(s) and all their messages.
sidebar_delete_chat_confirm_desc: This action is irreversible and will delete its messages.
runstatus_error_orphaned: The reply was generated on the agent side but could not be finalized (the bridge connection dropped mid-stream). You can delete or regenerate this message.
advfilter_empty_state: No criteria. Add a condition (all conditions are combined with AND).
bridgebadge_no_reading_yet: Bridge: no reading yet (polled every minute)
bridgebadge_unavailable: Bridge unavailable ({reason}) — open the Bridge tab for details
transcript_truncation_note: [Export limited to the 200 most recent messages of this conversation]
search_min_length_hint: Type at least {count} characters to search.
agentpicker_dialog_description: Select the agent (and instance) for this conversation.
agentpicker_empty_state: No agent is assigned to you. Contact your administrator to have one assigned.
agentpicker_agent_deleted_title: This agent no longer exists on the gateway
error_agent_not_found_hint: The configured agent no longer exists on the gateway. Check OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID in the bridge .env (it must match a real gateway agent).
error_auth_token_mismatch_hint: The operator token does not match the paired device. Re-pair a dedicated bridge device on the gateway and report its token + device identity.
error_device_signing_failed_hint: The device identity private key won’t decode. Check OPENCLAW_DEVICE_IDENTITY (JSON format, single \n) — see fix-devid.mjs.
error_session_scope_denied_hint: The device pairing lacks the required scope (operator.admin). Approve/elevate the device scope on the gateway.
error_gateway_timeout_hint: The gateway did not respond in time. Check it’s started and reachable (OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL).
error_gateway_disconnected_hint: The connection to the gateway dropped. Check the OpenClaw container state and the gateway URL/port.
error_bridge_unreachable_hint: Convex could not reach the bridge. Check the bridge container is running and BRIDGE_URL (http://<host-ip>:8787).
error_attachment_too_large_hint: The gateway refused the attachment because it exceeds the size it accepts (the agent's sandbox staging limit). Use a smaller file, or remove the attachment and send the text alone.
error_attachment_rejected_hint: The gateway could not parse/stage the attachment. Known gateway bug (its base64 validator overflows on large files — RangeError); fix it gateway-side (isValidBase64). Meanwhile: a smaller file works, or send the text without the attachment.
error_invalid_request_hint: The gateway rejected the request. Check the bridge logs for the raw detail.
error_not_configured_hint: BRIDGE_URL / BRIDGE_SHARED_SECRET are not set on the Convex side (npx convex env set).
error_unrouted_hint: This account is not attached to any instance. Settings → Users → Override instance, or assign a group.
error_upstream_error_hint: Uncategorized cause. Check the bridge logs for the raw message.
error_unknown_hint: No cause code was reported (bridge older than this version?). Check the bridge logs.
error_uncategorized_hint: Cause not catalogued — see the bridge logs.
notif_feedback_reply_body: An administrator replied to a report you sent.
access_hint: Inspect a user’s access: their groups, the agents and charts available to them (with provenance), and their effective permissions. Read-only.
access_empty_pick: Select a user to see their access.
groups_hint: Group users and share agents by group. Each member inherits the group's agents on top of their direct agents.
groups_sheet_desc: The name is free-form; a stable identifier is generated automatically.
groups_delete_confirm_desc: This deletes the group, its memberships and shared agents. It cannot be undone.
groups_manage_desc: Add or remove members and share discovered agents with this group.
groups_charts_pool_empty: No charts available for this group yet. Ask an admin to add charts to this group's pool.
groups_charts_pool_admin_empty: Add charts to this group's pool below, then select which ones the group offers.
groups_charts_pool_add: Add a chart to the pool…
spanel_verbosity_help: Pinned by the bridge for complete streaming.
spanel_compact_confirm_desc: The agent summarizes the conversation to free space in the context window. Messages stay visible here.
spanel_reset_confirm_desc: The agent session starts fresh and re-hydrates from the conversation on the next message. No message is deleted.
afiles_desc: The agent’s workspace files (rules, identity, tools). The gauge tracks each file’s bootstrap budget usage; writing is admin-only and every write is audited.
afiles_error_bridge: Bridge unreachable — cannot list the files.
afiles_source_note: Raw markdown source — a rendered preview is not available here.
afiles_conflict: The file changed since it was loaded. Reload before saving (your edits are kept).
afiles_confirm_desc: The file will be written to agent {agent}. A full before/after revision is kept.
cdefaults_desc: Default settings for new chat sessions, read from the gateway’s global configuration. Per-chat and per-agent settings keep precedence.
cdefaults_thinking_help: Reasoning level applied when a chat does not set its own.
cdefaults_speed_help: Fast or standard mode applied when a chat does not set its own.
cdefaults_error_bridge: Bridge unreachable — cannot read the global defaults.
cdefaults_save_error: Failed to write the global defaults.
cdefaults_note: These values are written to openclaw.json, the instance’s global configuration — they apply to every user.
cdefaults_confirm_desc: This write changes openclaw.json for the whole instance: every chat inheriting the default will change behavior.
compat_no_data: No compatibility reading yet — the poll runs every 5 minutes.
compat_stale: Last poll failed — showing the last good reading.
compat_legacy_manifest: This bridge does not publish a compatibility manifest (older version) — legacy policy: only model and reasoning are offered.
compat_no_targets: No connection known to the compatibility reading.
compat_unsupported_instance: Not supported by this instance (gateway version {version}).
compat_unsupported_instance_unknown: Not supported by this instance (unknown gateway version).
subagent_panel_jump_hint: Go to the main agent's message that spawned this sub-agent
subagent_bar_context_fork_hint: "fork" context: the parent agent's transcript was branched into the sub-agent
subagent_interact_archived: Session archived after its reply (cleanup "delete") — interaction unavailable
subagent_interact_wait_running: Wait for the sub-agent to finish
assistant_empty_waiting: The agent delegated to a sub-agent — waiting for its result…
assistant_empty_waiting_named: The agent delegated to a sub-agent ("{task}") — waiting for its result…
assistant_empty_failed: The agent delegated to a sub-agent that failed: {reason}
assistant_empty_failed_named: The agent delegated to a sub-agent ("{task}") that failed: {reason}
assistant_empty_failed_hint: You can send a new message to continue.
assistant_empty_generic: The agent performed some actions but did not return a response.
integrations_otlp_note: Export Atrium's traces to any OpenTelemetry/OTLP backend (Grafana Tempo, Jaeger, Datadog, an OTEL collector). Everything is configured here — no env vars.
integrations_otlp_headers_hint: Auth goes in the headers, never in the URL. Headers are encrypted at rest.
subagentreport_desc: Freeze a snapshot of "{label}" and its context so an administrator can analyze what went wrong.
subagentreport_submitted: Snapshot frozen — an administrator can now analyze this failure.
subagentreport_cat_wrong_result: It finished but the result was wrong
subagentreport_comment_placeholder: Add any detail that could help debug this…
sareports_hint_prefix: The list shows metadata only. Opening a report's detail reads the frozen snapshot and is
sareports_hint_suffix: as a cross-user content access. The content-free counterpart of each report is in the Anomalies tab.
sareports_delete_confirm_description: The frozen snapshot will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.
charts_bpm_help: 0 = no motion. Otherwise the chart's ambient aura beats at this tempo (requires ambient effects on).
